base_model_summary.architecture: MoE + MiniMax Sparse Attention + multimodal model

quantized_options_found.notes: GGUF support is preliminary; MiniMax Sparse Attention not supported in released llama.cpp path and falls back to dense attention.
Quantized MLX repos exist, but 428B-param class 4-bit weights exceed 48GB by a wide margin before KV/cache/runtime overhead.
External-runtime candidate only; not a native BloomBee block-serving proof and not runnable on current macOS/MPS hosts.

conclusion: Quantized MiniMax-M3 is worth tracking, but current M4 16GB + M4 Pro 48GB cannot host even the reported smallest MiniMax-M3 quant. The next practical MiniMax-M3 gate remains external-runtime smoke on a >=128GB RAM/VRAM machine for 1-bit GGUF, or approved multi-GPU NVIDIA/AMD runtime for MXFP/NVFP/AWQ paths.
recommended_next_steps: Do not churn local macOS downloads for MiniMax-M3; they will not fit.
If we want a local MiniMax-family experiment, scout MiniMax-M2.7 or smaller variants separately, not as MiniMax-M3 proof.
Update the existing external-runtime smoke plan with a quantized GGUF path guarded by >=128GB host requirement and a vLLM/SGLang path guarded by approved GPU runtime requirement.
